From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2008 British television comedy-drama series

| *Honest* | |
| --- | --- |
| Created by | Jack Williams |
| Developed by | Greenlit Rights |
| Written by | James Griffin [Rachel Lang](/wiki/Rachel_Lang "Rachel Lang") |
| Starring | [Amanda Redman](/wiki/Amanda_Redman "Amanda Redman") [Sean Pertwee](/wiki/Sean_Pertwee "Sean Pertwee") [Matthew McNulty](/wiki/Matthew_McNulty "Matthew McNulty") Eleanor Wyld [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock") [Danny Webb](/wiki/Danny_Webb_(British_actor) "Danny Webb (British actor)") Amit Shah |
| Country of origin | United Kingdom |
| Original language | English |
| No. of series | 1 |
| No. of episodes | 6 |
| Production | |
| Executive producer | Jill Green |
| Producer | Eve Gutierrez |
| Camera setup | Rodrigo Gutierrez |
| Running time | 50 minutes |
| Original release | |
| Network | [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") |
| Release | 9 January (2008-01-09) – 13 February 2008 (2008-02-13) |

***Honest*** is a British [comedy](/wiki/Comedy "Comedy")-[drama](/wiki/Drama "Drama") [series](/wiki/Television_program "Television program") that aired on [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") in 2008. The series is a [remake](/wiki/Remake "Remake") of the [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") series *[Outrageous Fortune](/wiki/Outrageous_Fortune_(TV_series) "Outrageous Fortune (TV series)")*, written by James Griffin and [Rachel Lang](/wiki/Rachel_Lang "Rachel Lang"), that first aired in 2005.

The programme stars [Amanda Redman](/wiki/Amanda_Redman "Amanda Redman") as Lindsay Carter, a mother of four who decides that her criminal family is going to change its ways after her husband is sent to prison. *Honest* also stars [Danny Webb](/wiki/Danny_Webb_(British_actor) "Danny Webb (British actor)"), [Sean Pertwee](/wiki/Sean_Pertwee "Sean Pertwee") and [Camille Coduri](/wiki/Camille_Coduri "Camille Coduri").

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Honest_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Production")]

Most of the filming was carried out in [Staines](/wiki/Staines "Staines"), including at the local school, [Matthew Arnold](/wiki/Matthew_Arnold_School_(Staines) "Matthew Arnold School (Staines)").

## Broadcast Dates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Honest_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Broadcast Dates")]

The first episode aired on Wednesday 9 January 2008 at 21:00. It pulled in an average audience of 6 million viewers, winning the 21:00 slot with a 24% share.[[1]](#cite_note-1) However, the second episode figures fell to 4.3 million viewers (17%) [[2]](https://www.theguardian.com/media/2008/jan/17/tvnews.tvratings), in competition with the second season premier of [Torchwood](/wiki/Torchwood "Torchwood") on the BBC, and the third episode declined further to 3.7 million viewers (15%) [[3]](https://www.theguardian.com/media/2008/jan/24/tvratings.television). Episode 4 saw an increase of one percentage point from the previous week to 3.9 million viewers (16%) [[4]](https://www.theguardian.com/media/2008/jan/31/tvratings.television). However Episode 5 dropped to 3.2 million viewers, reflecting direct competition with [Fabio Capello](/wiki/Fabio_Capello "Fabio Capello")'s debut as England football manager on [Match of the Day](/wiki/Match_of_the_Day "Match of the Day")[[5]](https://www.theguardian.com/media/2008/feb/07/tvratings.television). The final installment saw figures rally by 800,000 to 4 million, an increase of 17% taking second place in the 9pm slot to Channel 4's [Grand Designs](/wiki/Grand_Designs "Grand Designs") [[6]](https://www.theguardian.com/media/2008/feb/14/tvratings.channel4). The figures average at 4.18 million per week.

## DVD releases

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Honest_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: DVD releases")]

*Honest* was released on DVD in Region 2 on 18 February 2008.[[2]](#cite_note-Amazon-2)
